food_subgroup: Crustaceans — mixed prawn and crab dish with spiced sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined prawn-and-crab dish with chili rempah and assam aioli. FOODON_03311737 (shrimp food product) is used as the closest parent for the prawn component. Crab component is not separately anchored in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJM (Crustaceans, cooked) is the closest defensible parent for this mixed prawn-and-crab preparation. No specific code exists for the combined dish with chili rempah and assam aioli condiments.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227144002 (Crustacean - food) is used as the closest parent. No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for this combined Singaporean-style prawn and crab dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of prawn and crab with chili rempah and assam aioli is approximately 250 g, comprising a mix of whole or halved crustaceans, sauce, celery, and peanut garnish.

nutrient_source.title: Cooked Prawns / Shrimp and Cooked Crab — composite estimate from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries (Shrimp, cooked, NDB 15152; Crab, cooked, NDB 15139) weighted 60:40 prawn:crab, adjusted for chili-rempah sauce and aioli contribution using HPB Singapore nutrient reference data for chili-based sauces
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central; Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific restaurant dish. Values are estimated from a 60:40 weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy cooked shrimp (NDB 15152, 99 kcal/100 g) and cooked crab (NDB 15139, 97 kcal/100 g), then adjusted upward for the caloric contribution of chili rempah paste (~15 g/100 g dish) and assam aioli (~10 g/100 g dish) and peanut garnish (~5 g/100 g dish). Final composite per-100 g values are modelled estimates. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — seafood and shellfish category; analogous estimate from Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific prawn-and-crab dish. Seafood and crustaceans are consistently low-GI foods. A GI of 16 is used as an analogous estimate based on published GI values for cooked shrimp and mixed seafood dishes (GI range 5–20) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 (Diabetes Care). Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as (16 × 4.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 2.5) / 100 = 1.9 g. This is a low-GL food.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from chili rempah paste and aioli
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 420 mg sodium per 100 g (1050 mg per 250 g serving), this dish contributes a substantial portion of the 1500–2000 mg daily sodium target for hypertensive individuals; patients should request reduced-sauce portions or limit serving frequency.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and high protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 16 and only 4.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g, this dish has a negligible glycemic load and is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-carbohydrate accompaniments such as white rice or noodles.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol content from crustaceans and aioli, partially offset by omega-3 fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Prawns and crab are naturally high in dietary cholesterol (~145 mg/100 g) but low in saturated fat; the omega-3 content is cardioprotective, though the aioli and rempah add saturated fat and sodium, making portion control important for individuals with established cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prawn and Crab with Chili Rempah and Assam Aioli is a Singaporean-Chinese fusion seafood dish served at Dumpling Darlings on Circular Road, Singapore. The dish features whole or halved prawns and crab pieces stir-fried or tossed in a chili rempah — a fragrant spiced paste made from dried and fresh chilies, shallots, garlic, galangal, and lemongrass — and finished with an assam (tamarind-based) aioli that provides a tangy, creamy counterpoint to the heat of the rempah. Celery adds crunch and freshness, while crushed peanuts provide a nutty garnish. The flavour profile is bold, spicy, tangy, and umami-rich, with the natural sweetness of fresh crustaceans balanced by the acidity of tamarind and the richness of the aioli. Nutritionally, the dish is a high-protein, moderate-fat, low-carbohydrate preparation. Per 100 g it provides approximately 138 kcal, 13.5 g protein, 7.2 g fat, and only 4.8 g carbohydrate, giving it a very low glycemic index (estimated GI ~16) and negligible glycemic load. It is a good source of vitamin B12, zinc, selenium, and phosphorus from the crustaceans. Sodium is elevated due to the rempah paste and aioli, making portion awareness important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is not suitable for those with shellfish, peanut, or egg allergies.

Is this dish suitable for diabetics?
Prawn and crab GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy cooked shrimp and cooked crab entries, adjusted for sauce and condiment contributions. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cooked crustaceans. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level data from USDA SR Legacy is high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source databases (USDA SR Legacy, HPB) for this type of composite dish. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in cooked crustacean and plant-oil-based sauce preparations per USDA SR Legacy — confirmed zero by source data. Serving size of 250 g is an estimate based on typical Singapore restaurant portion sizes for a shared seafood plate; actual restaurant portion may vary. Halal status is marked false because the aioli contains eggs and the dish origin and preparation method at this specific restaurant have not been verified against MUIS halal certification. The dish is served at Dumpling Darlings, Circular Road, Singapore (field survey data listing). Nutrient values are modelled estimates and should be replaced with laboratory analysis or an exact HPB/USDA match if one becomes available.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed. Identified that food_name needed to be more descriptive than the bare input 'Prawn and Crab' to satisfy Q1 canonical identity requirement. Ensured all five Singaporean-cuisine mandatory languages were present in local_names and aliases_by_language.

Iteration 2: Addressed g3_numeric_hygiene hard gate failure by explicitly documenting the reason for `per_100g.vitamin_d_mcg = -1` in the `notes` field, clarifying it is not reported in primary sources for this composite dish. Confirmed all other hard gates and Q-criteria remain passing.
